intro: The prophecy of the Seventy Weeks (Dan. 9:24–27) was revealed to Daniel by the angel Gabriel in response to Daniel's prayer of repentance over Jeremiah's prophecy of seventy years of exile. Gabriel declared that seventy "weeks" (Hebrew shavuim, "sevens" — commonly interpreted as weeks of years, yielding 490 years) were determined upon Daniel's people and holy city for six specified purposes: to finish transgression, make an end of sins, make atonement for iniquity, bring in everlasting righteousness, seal up vision and prophecy, and anoint a most holy place. The decree is subdivided into 7 + 62 + 1 weeks, with a gap implied before the final week. The starting point of the decree, the identity of the anointed prince, and the precise referent of the final week have generated extensive debate among preterist, historicist, and futurist interpreters, making it one of the most discussed prophetic passages in Scripture.
